company_type: Water Heating and Treatment Products Manufacturing

dependencies: Dependence on limited number of suppliers for certain raw materials and components
risks: Material and component price volatility, particularly steel
Tariffs could increase volatility in steel and other input materials
Supplier concentration - dependent on limited number of suppliers for some raw materials and components
Significant disruption or termination of supply from key suppliers could delay sales or increase costs
International operations subject to supply chain disruptions

regulatory_risks: Department of Energy (DOE) efficiency rules for commercial water heaters effective 2026
DOE efficiency rules for residential water heaters effective 2029
New national drinking water standards regulating PFAS and lead
State and local government bans on gas-fired products in new construction to address greenhouse gas emissions
Federal, state and local environmental laws and regulations
Occupational Health and Safety Administration regulations
Foreign government regulations in countries where products are manufactured, distributed and sold
Laws requiring transition to non-fossil fuel based energy sources
Regulations significantly reducing or eliminating on-site combustion of fossil fuels in building sector
Laws prohibiting improper payments to government officials
Laws restricting business activities in certain countries
Laws restricting information sharing with non-U.S. governments

sanction_risks: Import tariffs, taxes, customs duties imposed by U.S. government on foreign countries
Retaliatory tariffs from other countries on U.S.
Current U.S. administration desire to impose substantial new or increased tariffs
Trade restrictions and changes to trade agreements
